caption:
Comment “JAPAN” for a Google Map link of the store!

If money is no object, this should be your first stop for gifts while you’re in Tokyo. 

Located in the heart of Harajuku, this is where you’ll find some of the coolest handmade products that are normally too hard to find or just not easy to get anywhere else. 

This company is famously known for producing and sourcing made in Japan products, which means you’ll find incredible artisan pieces you won’t see anywhere else. 

From @a.craft86 x @sashiko_gals daruma, hand crafted kendama, and @studiodartisan / @yamane.industries.shop denim, you’ll find one of a kind pieces at this store. 
—
IG: @japan_edition_official
HARAJUKU: 12PM-8PM
KYOTO: 11AM-8PM

#tokyo #craftsman #daruma #kendama #denim
transcript: If money is no object, this is a shop to visit for Made in Japan gifts. Today, we're here at Japan Edition, which is part of Tokyo Base, who runs some of my favorite clothing companies like Public Tokyo and Studius. Focusing on Made in Japan, this store is a treasure trove of handcrafted Japanese products. On the first floor, you'll find this handmade Daruma made by aircraft and sashiko gals, kendama, mini choching, and bonsai trees that are all works of art. You'll find other items like sunglasses, figurines, traditional Japanese bowls, watches, and more, of course, all from Japan. Let's go to the second floor where you'll find more fashion and accessories, including goyemo senta sandals and OAO shoes that you guys seen before. Of course, you can't forget about denim when you're in Japan, and they carry Studio Dardison and Yamane Industries. You'll be spending a pretty penny here, but you'll definitely get what you pay for, happiness and a piece of Japan. Thanks for watching, and if you love fashion and Japan, don't forget to follow.
